approval.note: Do not add a backend merely for convenience. Compare privacy, abuse handling, operational burden, retention and traceability against the existing public GitHub path.

metadata.observed_limit: The preferred GitHub issue path is public and account-bound; the local JSON fallback does not transmit itself.
does_not_establish: need for a Commonworld account system
permission to collect unnecessary contact data

title: Evaluate a lower-barrier and privacy-capable Commons proposal channel
goal: Determine whether Commonworld needs an additional non-public or lower-barrier submission path, and adopt it only if it improves access without weakening spam control, traceability, data minimisation or the static publication boundary.

acceptance.assertion: The decision uses observed proposal-path use, abandonment and privacy needs rather than hypothetical demand alone.
GitHub, email, serverless form, structured download and a small backend are compared for privacy, spam, retention, accessibility, cost and editorial traceability.
Any adopted path collects only necessary data, states retention and deletion rules and keeps contact data out of the public catalogue.
No alternative channel can publish a CommonProject without the existing editorial and repository review boundary.
An adopted path has tested abuse limits, failure states, monitoring, accessibility and a documented shutdown or migration path.
